summary: Mutation testing results: 68 killed, 17 survived, 0 no coverage (80% mutation score)
testQuality: The Push v2 schemas are well-tested with a mutation score of 80%. The 17 surviving mutants fall into two categories: (1) error message string literals that tests do not assert on (expected — Zod error messages are implementation details), and (2) v1 schema enum values and regex anchors that are not directly exercised by push-schemas.test.ts. The v1 schemas (phaseSchema, entityStatusSchema, documentTypeSchema, dateStringSchema, kebabIdSchema) are covered by the validator test suite but those tests do not enumerate all enum values. The 80% threshold is met and the surviving mutants are low-risk.

suggestions.description: Mutants survived: 'development', 'testing', 'review', 'done' in phaseSchema each individually replaceable with empty string
suggestions.rationale: The push-schemas.test.ts file does not test the v1 phaseSchema directly. Only 'ideation' is implicitly tested by the featureFrontmatterSchema. The validator tests cover more values but mutation analysis shows gaps for 'development', 'testing', 'review', 'done'.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a direct test for phaseSchema: it('accepts all valid phases', () => { for (const p of ['ideation', 'development', 'testing', 'review', 'done']) { expect(phaseSchema.parse(p)).toBe(p); } }); it('rejects invalid phase', () => { expect(() => phaseSchema.parse('sprint')).toThrow(); });

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: 'completed' in entityStatusSchema replaceable with empty string
suggestions.rationale: The projectEntitySchema tests use 'not_started' and 'in_progress' but never 'completed', leaving 'completed' unkilled.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test in projectEntitySchema that uses status: 'completed', or add a direct entityStatusSchema test that iterates all three values.

suggestions.description: Mutants survived: 'epic', 'feature', 'fix' in documentTypeSchema each individually replaceable with empty string, and entire array replaceable with []
suggestions.rationale: documentTypeSchema is not tested directly by push-schemas.test.ts, and the validator tests do not kill these specific string-literal mutations in the enum.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a direct test: it('accepts all document types', () => { for (const t of ['epic', 'feature', 'fix']) { expect(documentTypeSchema.parse(t)).toBe(t); } }); it('rejects invalid type', () => { expect(() => documentTypeSchema.parse('story')).toThrow(); });

suggestions.description: Regex mutants survived: start anchor ^ and end anchor $ can each be removed from dateStringSchema without any test failing
suggestions.rationale: No test passes a string that matches the date pattern in the middle (e.g., 'prefix-2026-04-22') or that has trailing content (e.g., '2026-04-22T00:00:00Z'), so anchor removal is not caught.
suggestions.suggestion: Add tests: expect(() => dateStringSchema.parse('prefix-2026-04-22')).toThrow(); expect(() => dateStringSchema.parse('2026-04-22T00:00:00Z')).toThrow(); expect(() => dateStringSchema.parse(' 2026-04-22')).toThrow();

suggestions.description: Regex mutants survived: end anchor $ and * quantifier can be removed from kebabIdSchema regex
suggestions.rationale: Without the $ anchor, a string like 'valid-id trailing garbage' would pass. Without *, only a single segment kebab-id would be required but multi-segment IDs would still pass (the quantifier change makes '-[a-z0-9]+' non-repeating, so 'a-b-c' would fail but 'a-b' would still pass).
suggestions.suggestion: Add tests: expect(() => kebabIdSchema.parse('valid-id trailing')).toThrow(); expect(kebabIdSchema.parse('a-b-c')).toBe('a-b-c'); — the multi-segment test kills the * removal.
